data.title: Add manifest_depth field to complexity-evaluator agent output schema
data.description:
## Testing Mode
GREEN

## Context
Story 86ad-f5e7. The complexity-evaluator agent currently outputs TRIVIAL/MODERATE/COMPLEX as `classification`. This task adds `manifest_depth` to the output schema so callers can pass the depth tag directly to `_write_preconditions()` without reimplementing the mapping.

**Important design note**: manifest_depth is derived from classification; it does NOT replace it. Existing callers (sprint, fix-bug, brainstorm) that only read `classification` are unaffected — this is a purely additive field.

**Spike note**: This is a heuristic mapping. The mapping is TRIVIAL→minimal, MODERATE→standard, COMPLEX→deep. Story 6 FP auto-fallback hedges misclassification.

## Implementation

Edit: `plugins/dso/agents/complexity-evaluator.md`

Changes to output schema JSON block:
1. Add `manifest_depth` field: `"manifest_depth": "minimal|standard|deep"` (required field in output JSON)
2. Add mapping rules after existing classification rules:
   - TRIVIAL/SIMPLE → manifest_depth="minimal"
   - MODERATE → manifest_depth="standard"  
   - COMPLEX → manifest_depth="deep"
3. Add field to Rules section: `manifest_depth` MUST match the table above; never emit manifest_depth independently of classification

No other files need changes in this task — the mapping logic in preconditions-depth-classifier.sh (T2) is separate from the evaluator's self-declared schema.

## Unit Test Exemption Justification

Testing mode is GREEN because:
1. The change is to an LLM instruction file (complexity-evaluator.md) — no conditional logic in the file itself
2. A test that verifies the field is present in the markdown would be a change-detector test (existence-only assertion)
3. This is an instruction-file type: per behavioral-testing-standard.md Rule 5, test the structural boundary (the field appears in the doc) rather than content
4. Behavioral testing of manifest_depth emission is covered by integration in T1 (the classifier tests verify the mapping)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `manifest_depth` field appears in complexity-evaluator.md output schema JSON block
  Verify: grep -q '"manifest_depth"' $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/plugins/dso/agents/complexity-evaluator.md
- [ ] Mapping rules (TRIVIAL→minimal, MODERATE→standard, COMPLEX→deep) documented
  Verify: grep -q "manifest_depth.*minimal\|minimal.*TRIVIAL" $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/plugins/dso/agents/complexity-evaluator.md
- [ ] check-plugin-self-ref.sh passes (no hardcoded plugins/dso/ paths in scripts)
  Verify: bash $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/plugins/dso/hooks/pre-commit/check-plugin-self-ref.sh

## Retry Budget
MAX_ATTEMPTS: 3 (sonnet model)
On 3 consecutive sonnet failures: escalate to opus with full diagnostic context (all 3 failure messages)
On 3 consecutive opus failures (6 total): escalate to user with full failure history
If MAX_AGENTS: 0 at sonnet→opus escalation time: skip opus step, escalate to user immediately
